learning_outcomes:
Outcomes 1.  |  Major programme:
Upon completion of this Major Programme, graduates will have
acquired the following learning outcomes in three aspects:
Knowledge
outcomes:
(1)  |  Different
forms of cultural practices, expressions, and meanings
(2)  |  Meanings
of collective identity, taste, and values
(3)  |  The
economic aspects of cultural production, circulation, and consumption
(4)  |  Community
preservation and regeneration
(5)  |  Importance
of cultural diversity and cultural participation
(6)  |  Art
history with a focus on the arts of Hong Kong, China, and Asia
(7)  |  Cultural
history and heritage of Hong Kong, China, and Asia
(8)  |  Basic
knowledge of proposal writing and arts festivals programming
(9)  |  Basic
knowledge in digital humanities
(10)  |  Basic
knowledge in public humanities
(11)  |  Basic
knowledge in arts tech
Skills outcomes:
(1)  |  Arts
appreciation
(2)  |  Organization
and management skills in working with different parties
(3)  |  Critical
skills in cultural interpretation
(4)  |  Basic
media skills, writing skills and design skills
(5)  |  Curatorial
planning
(6)  |  Research
skills to develop cultural policy
(7)  |  Interviewing
skills
(8)  |  Development
of oral history
(9)  |  Analysis
of non-tangible cultural heritage
Values and
attitude outcomes:
(1)  |  Importance
of diversity and pluralism in society
(2)  |  Intercultural
sensitivity and respect
(3)  |  A
balance of local, national, and global perspectives
(4)  |  Critical
awareness related to issues pertaining to gender, racial, class, and national
identities
(5)  |  Literacy
in different forms of visual and verbal expressions, understanding how
meanings and affects are constructed in different media forms and genres
(6)  |  Ability
to draw on a number of disciplinary origins within areas of humanities and
social sciences to appreciate the richness and the usefulness of interdisciplinary
approaches
(7)  |  Commitment
to developing a more inclusive and caring society
study_scheme:
Public Humanities Public Humanities Applicable to students admitted in
2025-26
Major Programme Requirement
Students
are required to complete a minimum of 66 units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Faculty Package:  |  9
CURE1000 or 1400, and any two courses from:
ANTH1020, ARTS1005, 1010,
BMBL1001, CHES1101 or 1102, CHLL1900 or 1902 or 1903, CURE1110, ENGE1000,
FAAS1900, HIST1000 or 1700 or 1701, JASP1090, LING1000, MUSC1000,
PHIL1110, THEO1000, TRAN1000
2.  |  Required
Courses:  |  18
(a)  |  CURE1004, 1009, 1401, 1403, 2001
(b)  |  Capstone Course: CURE4014 or 4038
3.  |  Elective Courses:  |  39
(a)  |  Any three courses from the
following: CURE2006, 2411, 2412, 3007, 4008,
4401
(b)  |  Nine courses at 2000 or above level
from Cultural Studies Areas and Cultural Management Areas, with at least
seven CURE courses, excluding the courses taken to fulfill other Major
Programme Requirements
(c)  |  One course from Religious Studies
Areas
Concentrations Students may choose to declare up to
one concentration by taking the following elective courses in place of 3.
Elective Courses above.
Cultural Management
(a)  |  CURE2411,
2412, 4401
(b)  |  Nine courses at 2000 or above level
from Cultural Management Areas, with at least seven CURE courses, excluding
the courses taken to fulfill other Major Programme Requirements
(c)  |  One course
from Religious Studies Areas
Cultural
Studies
(a)  |  CURE2006, 3007,
4008
(b)  |  Nine courses at 2000 or above level
from Cultural Studies Areas, with at least seven CURE courses, excluding
the courses taken to fulfill other Major Programme Requirements
(c)  |  One course
from Religious Studies Areas
Total:  |  66
Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  ANTH,
COMM, CURE, FAAS, HIST courses at 2000 and above level will be included
in the calculation of Major GPA for honours classification.
2.  |  Courses which count towards the
fulfillment of the elective course requirements specified in 3(a) and
concentrations (a) cannot be counted towards the fulfillment of the
elective course requirements specified in 3(b) and concentrations (b).

Major Programme Requirement (for
Associate Degree or Higher Diploma holders admitted to senior-year places)
Students are required to complete a
minimum of 51 units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Required Courses:  |  18
(a)  |  CURE1004, 1009, 1401, 1403, 2001
(b)  |  Capstone course: CURE4014 or 4038
2.  |  Elective
Courses:
(a)  |  Any three courses from the
following: CURE2006,
2411, 2412, 3007, 4008, 4401  |  9
(b)  |  Eight courses at 2000 or above level from
Cultural Studies Areas and Cultural
Management Areas, with at least
seven CURE courses, excluding the courses taken to fulfill
other Major Programme Requirements  |  24
Total:  |  51
Explanatory Note:
1.  |  ANTH, COMM, CURE, FAAS, HIST courses
at 2000 and above level will be included in the calculation of Major GPA
for honours classification.
Recommended Course Pattern (for
Associate Degree or Higher Diploma holders admitted to senior-year places)  |  Units

3007, 4008, 4401 Major Elective(s): 0-1 course from CURE4014 or 4038, and/or 1-4 courses  |  3-9 3-15
Total:  |  51
Minor Programme Title Cultural Studies
Minor Programme Requirement
Students are required to complete a
minimum of 18 units of courses, with at least 6
units at 3000 or above level as follows:
Units
1.  |  Elective Courses:
(a)  |  Any one course from the following:  |  3
CURE1009, 2001, 2006, 3007, 4008
(b)  |  5 CURE courses at 2000 or above
level from Cultural Studies Area(s), with at least 2 courses at 3000 or
above level, excluding the course taken to fulfill requirement 1(a) above  |  15
Total:  |  18
Explanatory
Note:
1.  |  This Minor
Programme is not applicable to students who major in Public Humanities.
2.  |  No more
than 9 units of courses taken to fulfill the requirements of the
students’ Major Programme(s) and other Minor Programme(s) respectively
can be used to fulfill the requirement of this Minor Programme.
Minor Programme Title Film and Screen Studies
Minor Programme Requirement
Students are required to complete a
minimum of 18 units of courses, with at least 6 units at 3000 or above
level, as follows:
Units
1.  |  Required Course:
CURE2006  |  3
2.  |  Elective Courses:
Any 5 courses from the following,
with at least 3 CURE courses and at least 2 courses at 3000 or above
level: CURE2024, 2030, 2040, 2066, 3026,
3033, 3034, 3066,4021, 4022, 4027, 4033, CHES3101, 3102,
COMM2932, 3939 and JASP2200  |  15
Total:  |  18
Explanatory Note:
1.  |  This Minor
Programme is not applicable to students who major in Public Humanities.
2.  |  No more
than 9 units of courses taken to fulfill the requirements of the
students’ Major Programme(s) and other Minor Programme(s) respectively
can be used to fulfill the requirement of this Minor Programme.
Minor Programme Title Cultural Management
Minor Programme Requirement
Students are required to complete a minimum of 21
units of courses, with at least 6 units at 3000 or above
level, as follows:
Units
1.  |  Required Courses:  |  9
CURE1401, 1403, 4401
2.  |  Elective Courses:  |  12
(a)  |  Any 3 units from CURE2405, 2411, 2412, 3403, 3404
(b)  |  Any 9 units Cultural Management Area(a), with at
least 3 units at 3000 or above level [a]
Total:  |  21
Explanatory Note:
1.  |  This Minor Programme is not applicable to students who major
in Public Humanities.
2.  |  No more than 10 units of courses taken to fulfill the
requirements of the students’ Major Programme(s) and other Minor
Programme(s) respectively can be used to fulfill the requirement of this
Minor Programme.
[a]  |  Courses
that count towards the fulfilment of elective course requirements
specified in 2(a) cannot be counted towards the fulfilment of the
elective course requirements specified in 2(b).
Minor Programme
Title Curating and
Artistic Strategies
Minor Programme Requirement
Students are required to
complete a minimum of 18 units of courses as follows with at least 6 units at 3000 or above level:
Units
1.  |  Required Courses:  |  6
CURE2407, FAAS2117
2.  |  Elective Courses:  |  12
(a)  |  Any 2 courses from the following: CURE2411, 3404,
3405, 3408
(b)  |  Any 2 courses from the following[a]: FAAS2208, 3108,
3229, 4201
Total:  |  18
Explanatory Note:
1.  |  No more than 9 units of courses taken to fulfill the
requirements of the students’ Major Programme(s) and other Minor
Programme(s) respectively can be used to fulfill the requirement of this
Minor Programme.
[a]  |  Public Humanities major
students cannot take FAAS3108 and FAAS4201 to fulfill the elective course
requirements specified in 2(b).
